title: Academic Career
description: Served as Librarian at Sambalpur University, Lecturer of Odia at Bhadrak College and Paralakhemundi Government College, and rose to become Vice-Principal and Principal of BJB Evening College, Bhubaneswar. His career in education spanned decades, touching the lives of countless students.

title: Ravenshaw College Legacy
description: A graduate and post-graduate of the prestigious Ravenshaw College, Cuttack — one of Odisha's oldest and most respected institutions. He specialised in Odia language and literature, building the academic foundation for his towering literary career.

title: Mentoring Future Writers
description: As a professor and principal, he mentored generations of students and aspiring writers, passing on his deep understanding of Odia literary traditions and encouraging new voices in the language.

title: Jhankara Magazine (1965–1969)
description: As editor and writer of Jhankara, he used this literary magazine as a platform to publish philosophical essays on Sri Aurobindo and promote serious literary discourse in Odia. The magazine became a vital space for Odia intellectual life under his guidance.

title: Utkala Prasanga & Orissa Review (1975–1978)
description: He edited two influential periodicals — Utkala Prasanga and Orissa Review — that played a significant role in documenting Odisha's cultural, literary, and socio-political landscape during a formative period in the state's modern history.

title: Champion of Odia Literature
description: Through his editorial work, he provided a platform for emerging Odia writers and fostered literary criticism, helping to elevate the standards of Odia literature and bring it wider recognition within India's diverse literary landscape.

title: Translator of Sri Aurobindo's Savitri
description: He undertook the monumental task of translating Sri Aurobindo's epic poem Savitri: A Legend and a Symbol into Odia, making this foundational spiritual text accessible to Odia readers. This was one of the most significant translation projects in modern Odia literary history.

title: Philosophical Lectures
description: He was a renowned speaker who delivered talks and lectures on the philosophies of Sri Aurobindo, Ramakrishna Paramhansa, and Swami Vivekananda, bringing these spiritual teachings to wider audiences across Odisha.

title: Spiritual Literary Works
description: His spiritual writings include Chanakya Neeti Ratnamala, Prema Bhaktira Marmakatha, Sanatan Dharmara Sarakatha, and several other works that explored Indian philosophical and spiritual traditions through an Odia literary lens.

title: Stories Adapted to Film
description: Two films based on his short stories received National Film Awards, demonstrating the cinematic power of his storytelling and bringing Odia literature to national audiences through the medium of film.

title: Folk Theatre Adaptations
description: His work Biplobi Bihanga (also known as Bihanga Biplab) has been adapted into folk theatre, keeping Odia theatrical traditions alive and bringing his literary vision to rural audiences.

title: Enriching Odia Identity
description: Works like Ame Kie Ame Odia ("Who Are We? We Are Odia") reflect his deep commitment to Odia identity and culture, celebrating the language and traditions of Odisha for future generations.

title: Family Legacy of Service
description: His son Guruprasad Mohapatra (IAS, Padma Shri 2022) carried forward the family legacy of service — to the nation as a civil servant, and to literature by translating his father's stories into English, published by Sahitya Akademi in 2021.
